steps.title: Use the no-real-solutions condition
steps.content: A quadratic has no real solutions when the expression under the square root in the quadratic formula is negative. For $kx^2-56x+16=0$, that condition is $$(-56)^2-4(k)(16)\lt0.$$ Simplify: $$3136-64k\lt0$$ so $$k\gt 49.$$

steps.title: Pick the least integer
steps.content: $k$ must be an integer greater than $49$, so the least possible integer is $k=50$. Therefore 50 is correct.
